# llm.kittens

> Single-binary C++/CUDA trainer for GPT-2 / GPT-3 / Llama-3, mirroring
> [karpathy/llm.c](https://github.com/karpathy/llm.c) feature-for-feature
> but replacing tile-MMA kernels (GEMM, MHA, LayerNorm/RMSNorm where useful)
> with [ThunderKittens](https://github.com/HazyResearch/ThunderKittens).
> H100 (sm_90a) for optimized TK/model runtime evidence, BF16 only, in v1.
> Blackwell (`sm_100a`, `sm_103a`, `sm_120a`) is build-supported with CUDA
> correctness fallbacks for Hopper-only kernels.
>
> **In-flight port.** [`goal.md`](goal.md) is the authoritative TODO. Read it
> first before answering any "what is done / what is left" question.

## Status

- M1 (skeleton, verbatim ports, BF16 lock, dataset prep): ✅ done
- M2 (single-GPU GPT-2 forward): 🟡 partial — GPT-2 forward wrappers and
  `gpt2_validate` are compile-wired; H100 forward-loss validation still pending
- M3 (single-GPU GPT-2 backward + parity): 🟡 partial — GPT-2 backward
  kernels, AdamW, global norm, and the parity harness compile; H100 parity
  pending
- M4 (multi-GPU GPT-2 124M): 🟡 scripts + log verifier landed; H100 run pending
- M5 (GPT-3 + multi-node + ZeRO 2/3): 🟡 descriptor + scripts + GPT dry-runs
  landed; ZeRO-2 compile-wired; ZeRO-3 parameter-shard runtime path
  compile-wired; H100/NCCL validation pending
- M6 (Llama-3 1B): 🟡 converter + kernels + trainer loop + dataloaders/checkpoint-resume/logging
  compile; TK GQA forward + supported-shape bwd compile; TK tile-load RoPE
  compile-wired for supported shapes; H100 validation pending
- M7 (Llama-3 8B multi-node): 🟡 Slurm script + converter validation hooks
  landed; gated HF conversion/runtime validation pending
- M8 (polish + profiling): 🟡 docs + profile/log verifier wired; profile helper enforces the 70% tensor-util gate; parser threshold logic has a synthetic CSV check; GEMM epilogue compile/profile-wired; `ncu`/H100 validation pending

## Operational docs

- [README.md](README.md): project overview, current state snapshot, build
  instructions for what runs today
- [goal.md](goal.md): per-milestone, per-task TODO with checkboxes and links
  to upstream reference files
- [docs/README.md](docs/README.md): documentation index, routes by reader need
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md): three-layer rule
  (`train_*.cu` → `llmc/*.cuh` → `llmc/tk/*.cuh`), file-by-file map,
  TMA-alignment constraint, dependency Mermaid
- [docs/build-and-run.md](docs/build-and-run.md): toolchain, `TK_ROOT`,
  `make` targets, env-var switches, smoke-test output
- [docs/cli-reference.md](docs/cli-reference.md): full flag tables for
  `train_gpt2cu`, `train_llama3cu`, and the dev/ helper Python scripts
- [docs/validation-harness.md](docs/validation-harness.md): phase catalogue,
  success markers, validate-only modes, and required `goal-complete`
  thresholds for `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh`
- [docs/kernel-reference.md](docs/kernel-reference.md): per-kernel
  mapping table, shape constraints, current vs target state
- [docs/precision.md](docs/precision.md): BF16-only invariant and v2 plan
- [docs/multi-gpu.md](docs/multi-gpu.md): ZeRO-0/1/2/3, ZeRO-3 parameter shards, NCCL init paths,
  H100-pod env vars, per-script status
- [docs/llama3.md](docs/llama3.md): Llama-3 model spec, converter, kernels,
  launch scripts, and validation plan
- [docs/testing.md](docs/testing.md): test pyramid and tolerance plan
- [scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh](scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh): executable
  H100 validation checklist for the remaining `goal.md` runtime gates
- [docs/porting-notes.md](docs/porting-notes.md): wrapper-PR checklist and
  common gotchas
- [doc/README.md](doc/README.md): narrative kernel porting tutorial archive
- [docs/agents.md](docs/agents.md): repo-local agent skills index

## Code Highlights

- [Makefile](Makefile): sm_90a, C++20, BF16 (`-DENABLE_BF16`), no cuBLAS or
  cuDNN. `TK_ROOT` defaults to `../ThunderKittens`; NCCL can be discovered
  from system installs, selected with `NCCL_DIR`, or selected with an explicit
  `NCCL_INCLUDE_PATH` / `NCCL_LIB_PATH` pair.
- [train_gpt2.cu](train_gpt2.cu): GPT-2/GPT-3 entrypoint, compile-ready;
  runtime parity pending.
- [dev/cuda/gpt2_validate.cu](dev/cuda/gpt2_validate.cu): forward-only GPT-2
  loss gate against `gpt2_124M_debug_state.bin`; compiles via
  `make gpt2_validate`, runtime H100 validation pending.
- [train_llama3.cu](train_llama3.cu): Llama trainer loop, compile-ready with
  TK GQA forward plus supported-shape backward; CUDA fallback; model +
  per-rank optimizer/dataloader checkpoints with `-y 1` resume; rank-0
  `main.log` metric logging; H100 validation pending. Host-only dry-runs use
  `set_zero_configs` and report the validated local ZeRO shard parameter count.
- [dev/download_llama3.py](dev/download_llama3.py): gated HF converter wrapper
  for `llama3.1:8B`; validates generated checkpoint headers/payload sizes and
  can run `train_llama3cu -e CHECKPOINT -x 0` with `--cpp-validate`; it can
  also write a tiny deterministic BF16 checkpoint with
  `--write-synthetic-checkpoint`, and the H100 harness exposes that path as
  `llama-checkpoint-smoke`.
- [dev/validate_llama3_converter.py](dev/validate_llama3_converter.py):
  host-only Llama converter writer smoke; fills a tiny model with deterministic
  BF16 values, verifies `write_model` header/payload order, and dry-parses it
  with `train_llama3cu -x 0 -z 2`.
- [dev/validate_llama_checkpoint_artifacts.py](dev/validate_llama_checkpoint_artifacts.py):
  host-only Llama checkpoint/resume artifact checker; validates `DONE_*`, model
  checkpoint headers, and rank state headers for expected magic/version, step,
  rank, and process count; `--self-test` covers synthetic pass/fail artifact
  headers.
- [llmc/matmul.cuh](llmc/matmul.cuh): TK-backed forward for llm.c `(OC, C)`
  weights plus TK dInp/dWeight backward. Default bias/GELU stay separate;
  GPT-2 MLP up-projection can opt into the compile-wired TK bias+GELU epilogue
  with `-ge 1`. Accumulated dWeight uses aligned scratch for TK `A^T*B`, then
  adds into the gradient tensor; `test_matmul` covers epilogue, overwrite, and
  accumulated dWeight paths.
- [llmc/attention.cuh](llmc/attention.cuh): TK MHA forward + padded dispatch,
  TK MHA backward for supported GPT shapes, and slow CUDA backward fallback.
- [dev/cuda/test_attention.cu](dev/cuda/test_attention.cu): GPT MHA
  forward/backward smoke harness against an independent CPU reference; includes
  direct TK forward at T=192 and padded TK forward plus TK backward at T=256;
  compiles via `make test_attention`, runtime H100 validation pending.
- [llmc/layernorm.cuh](llmc/layernorm.cuh): TK LayerNorm forward and
  fused-residual forward for supported widths; backward keeps the llm.c
  accumulator pattern but uses TK warp-sum reductions.
- [dev/cuda/test_layernorm.cu](dev/cuda/test_layernorm.cu): GPT LayerNorm
  forward/fused/backward smoke harness against independent CPU references;
  compiles via `make test_layernorm`, runtime H100 validation pending.
- [llmc/rmsnorm.cuh](llmc/rmsnorm.cuh): Llama RMSNorm forward/fused-residual
  via TK where supported; CUDA backward baseline; `make test_rmsnorm` compiles
  a CPU-reference smoke harness.
- [llmc/rope.cuh](llmc/rope.cuh): TK RoPE forward/backward wrapper;
  `make test_rope` compiles a CPU-reference smoke harness.
- [llmc/attention_gqa.cuh](llmc/attention_gqa.cuh): GQA/RoPE dispatch with TK
  forward and supported-shape backward for H100 BF16 shapes, with slow CUDA
  fallback; TK-supported shapes rotate Q/K inside the tile-load path, while
  fallback shapes use fused materialization/unpermute, and the wrapper
  inverse-rotates packed `dQ`/`dK` gradients before writing `dinp`.
- [dev/cuda/test_attention_gqa.cu](dev/cuda/test_attention_gqa.cu): M6 GQA +
  RoPE forward/backward smoke harness against an independent CPU reference;
  includes explicit T=128 fallback-backward and T=256 TK-backward success
  markers, compiles via `make test_attention_gqa`, runtime H100 validation
  pending. The dedicated harness phase is `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh
  gqa-runtime`.
- [llmc/swiglu.cuh](llmc/swiglu.cuh): plain CUDA SwiGLU forward/backward;
  `make test_swiglu` compiles a CPU-reference smoke harness.
- [scripts/](scripts/): GPT-2/GPT-3/Llama launch scripts, syntax-checked;
  runtime H100/NCCL validation pending.
- [scripts/run_llama3_1B.sh](scripts/run_llama3_1B.sh): Llama-3 1B full-run
  target; defaults to Llama-tokenized FineWeb-edu 100B train/val paths and
  writes `run.log` launch metadata for the completion harness.
- [scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh](scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh): target-host
  validation harness for preflight, compile, launch/Python syntax, CUDA runtime
  probe, source-level CUDA/NCCL/ZeRO/GELU-epilogue/runtime-marker contract guards, synthetic and prepared-data artifact checks,
  CPU-only GQA/RoPE reference checks,
  dedicated GQA/RoPE CUDA runtime comparison,
  synthetic profile parser/threshold checks, synthetic training-log verifier
  checks, synthetic Llama converter writer smoke, synthetic Llama checkpoint parser smoke, starter-pack
  metadata/debug-state checks, smoke, GPT-2 parity, Llama resume, GPT/Llama
  host-only ZeRO layout dry-runs, optional GPT/Llama checkpoint dry-runs, bounded
  Llama-3 1B FineWeb-edu stability, Llama checkpoint artifact header checks,
  GPT-2 smoke/Llama resume/long-run rank-0 training-log verification, ZeRO
  guards, profiling, and full-run phases.
  Runtime phases assert explicit final markers such as `<binary> smoke OK`,
  `CUDA runtime check passed.`, `gpt2_validate OK`, and `test_gpt2cu OK`.
  `preflight` and `cuda-runtime` target H100/sm90-class devices by default.
  `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh blackwell-compile` builds model and smoke
  targets for Blackwell arch values, while `blackwell-device` and
  `rtx5090-device` run target-specific CUDA probes. These paths are not valid
  completion evidence for unchecked H100 gates. `ALLOW_NON_H100=1` remains a
  dry-debug override, and `goal-complete` refuses to run while it is set.
  `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh host-core` runs the non-CUDA-runtime host-side
  subset against existing built binaries/artifacts.
  `ALLOW_FULL_GOAL_RUN=1 scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh goal-complete` runs
  `goal-core` plus ZeRO-3 smoke and the long H100/NCCL/profile/conversion/full-run phases in one pass;
  it requires `GPT2_FULL_EXPECTED_VAL_LOSS` and `GPT2_FULL_EXPECTED_HELLASWAG`
  plus explicit two-node, smoke, ZeRO-3 smoke, and Llama thresholds, including
  `ZERO3_SMOKE_MAX_VAL_LOSS`, and fail-fast checks `ncu`,
  `gpt2_124M_bf16.bin`, `sbatch` when needed, and validate-only
  reference/candidate evidence files before `goal-core`; `goal-complete-prereqs`
  runs those checks without launching jobs. Thresholds are validated up front:
  losses and tolerances must be positive finite numbers, and HellaSwag
  thresholds must be finite values in `[0,1]`.
  Validate-only `zero3-smoke` also requires `ZERO3_SMOKE_RUN_LOG` with the
  ZeRO-3 stage banner, so a plain rank-0 metric log cannot stand in for a
  ZeRO-3 runtime smoke.
  Full-run validate-only gates require `run.log` launch metadata and final
  checkpoint markers/artifacts in addition to rank-0 `main.log` metrics.
  `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh gpt2-two-node` runs the filesystem Slurm
  script for 100 steps by default, then compares single-node vs two-node
  train-loss curves with `dev/compare_training_logs.py`, requiring both curves
  to decrease over the compared window.
  `GPT2_FULL_VALIDATE_ONLY=1` and `LLAMA1B_FULL_VALIDATE_ONLY=1` validate
  existing single-node full-run evidence instead of relaunching those phases.
  Short H100 gates can replay captured evidence with `PREFLIGHT_VALIDATE_ONLY`,
  `CUDA_RUNTIME_VALIDATE_ONLY`, `SMOKE_VALIDATE_ONLY`, `GPT2_RUNTIME_VALIDATE_ONLY`,
  `GQA_RUNTIME_VALIDATE_ONLY`,
  `GPT2_SMOKE_VALIDATE_ONLY`, `LLAMA_RESUME_VALIDATE_ONLY`, and
  `LLAMA1B_STABILITY_VALIDATE_ONLY`.
  `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh goal-replay-smoke` exercises those replay paths
  with synthetic temporary evidence and checks that weak prereq evidence is
  rejected.
  `goal-complete` forces Llama-3 1B stability HellaSwag on, and any
  `LLAMA1B_STABILITY_MIN_HELLASWAG` threshold requires final-step eval evidence.
  `LLAMA8B_CONVERT_VALIDATE_ONLY=1` requires an existing `LLAMA8B_CHECKPOINT`
  instead of attempting the gated HF conversion.
  `PROFILE_VALIDATE_ONLY=1 PROFILE_CSV_DIR=...` validates existing raw
  `profile_ge*.csv` exports without requiring Nsight Compute locally; use
  `PROFILE_REPORT_DIR=...` for `profile_ge*.ncu-rep` reports on a host with
  `ncu`. `goal-complete` forces/requires both `profile_ge0` and `profile_ge1`
  evidence, even if a standalone profile replay narrows `PROFILE_GELU_FUSIONS`.
  `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama8b-convert` validates or converts the real
  gated HF Llama-3.1 8B checkpoint and dry-parses it through the C++ ZeRO layout.
  `scripts/validate_goal_h100.sh llama8b-full` uses `sbatch --wait`, then checks
  the captured Slurm launch metadata plus
  the final 8B checkpoint headers and rank-0 training log.
- [dev/validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py](dev/validate_gpt2_starter_pack.py):
  host-only GPT-2 artifact checker for tokenizer, fp32/BF16 checkpoint sizes,
  and `gpt2_124M_debug_state.bin` shape/token/loss/sample validation;
  `--self-test` covers tiny synthetic starter-pack artifacts and expected failures.
- [dev/compare_training_logs.py](dev/compare_training_logs.py):
  host-only rank-0 `main.log` curve comparator for the M5 two-node sanity gate.
- [dev/validate_log_tools.py](dev/validate_log_tools.py):
  synthetic host-only pass/fail smoke for training-log expected metrics,
  final-step checks, and loss-curve validators.
- [dev/validate_launch_scripts.py](dev/validate_launch_scripts.py):
  source guard for single-node, multi-node, and PyTorch-reference launch-script
  `MAX_STEPS`, trainer step-count, and `DONE_*` alignment.
- [dev/validate_build_contracts.py](dev/validate_build_contracts.py):
  source guard for Makefile BF16/Hopper+Blackwell/TK invariants, BF16 `floatX`,
  TK bridge static guards, 128-byte alignment, dynamic smem opt-in, and empty
  cuBLAS shim.
- [dev/validate_epilogue_source.py](dev/validate_epilogue_source.py):
  source guard for the opt-in GPT-2 bias+GELU epilogue across the TK GEMM
  template, matmul wrapper, `-ge` routing/fallback, profile mode, launch
  scripts, and smoke.
- [dev/validate_gqa_source.py](dev/validate_gqa_source.py):
  source guard for GQA/RoPE tile-load routing, query-to-KV head mapping,
  supported-shape gates, TK backward RoPE gradient unpermute ordering, and
  T=128/T=256 smoke plus CPU reference coverage.
- [dev/validate_runtime_markers.py](dev/validate_runtime_markers.py):
  source guard for CUDA runtime, kernel-smoke, GPT-2 validation, and GQA runtime
  success markers asserted by the H100 harness.
- [dev/validate_training_source.py](dev/validate_training_source.py):
  source guard for rank-0 `main.log` evidence contracts across `logger.h`, both
  trainers, the log parser, and harness log-validation arguments.
- [dev/validate_profile_source.py](dev/validate_profile_source.py):
  source guard for `profile_gpt2cu.py`, the profiling binary, ncu raw metrics,
  tensor-utilization threshold, default/fused GELU modes, and harness `profile`
  phase wiring.
- [dev/validate_llama_conversion_source.py](dev/validate_llama_conversion_source.py):
  source guard for the gated Llama-3.1 8B HF alias, BF16 checkpoint validation,
  synthetic checkpoint path, C++ dry-parse options, and `llama8b-convert`.
- [dev/validate_goal_harness_coverage.py](dev/validate_goal_harness_coverage.py):
  source guard for compile targets, unchecked runtime evidence mapping,
  `goal-complete` phase coverage, completion prerequisites, replay negative
  evidence checks, and required explicit metric threshold environment variables.
- [dev/validate_data_artifacts.py](dev/validate_data_artifacts.py):
  host-only GPT-2/Llama train/eval `.bin` checker for magic/version, exact file
  sizes, token widths, sampled train-token ranges, and HellaSwag example streams;
  `--self-test` covers synthetic pass/fail train/eval artifacts.
- [dev/test_dataloader.cpp](dev/test_dataloader.cpp): host-only C++ smoke for
  GPT-2/Llama `DataLoader` and `EvalLoader` dispatch, rank offsets, shifted
  targets, eval labels, and masks; built with `make test_dataloader`.
- [dev/validate_attention_gqa_reference.py](dev/validate_attention_gqa_reference.py):
  CPU-only PyTorch reference equivalence for Llama GQA/RoPE at `B=1 T=128` and
  `B=1 T=256`, including packed Q/K/V backward gradients.
- [dev/validate_profile_parser.py](dev/validate_profile_parser.py): host-only
  synthetic Nsight Compute CSV parser and tensor-utilization threshold smoke.
- [dev/validate_training_log.py](dev/validate_training_log.py): host-only
  rank-0 `main.log` checker for GPT-2 smoke, Llama resume, and long training
  phases; validates final train/validation/eval steps, finite metrics, optional
  thresholds, and train-loss decrease where required.
- [dev/validate_nccl_source.py](dev/validate_nccl_source.py): host-only source
  guard that catches scalar NCCL all-reduce count regressions such as
  `sizeof(float)` used where NCCL expects an element count, and checks that
  optimizer shard all-gathers wait on compute-stream AdamW updates first. It
  also checks the ZeRO-3 parameter-shard buffer, shard-local AdamW update, and
  all-gather back into the full compute layout, including post-all-gather
  update synchronization before later full-layout reads.
- [dev/validate_zero_layout.py](dev/validate_zero_layout.py): host-only ZeRO
  shard offset checker for GPT-2, every built-in GPT-3 descriptor, and Llama-3
  1B/8B at 1/2/4/8/16 processes; `source-guards` runs it.
- [dev/data/](dev/data/): GPT-2 and Llama-3 dataset prep; Llama HellaSwag
  writes `hellaswag_val_llama3.bin`.
- [llmc/dataloader.h](llmc/dataloader.h): GPT-2 uint16 and Llama-3 uint32
  training/eval files dispatch by header magic.

## Verification Snapshot

- `make all NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0` passes.
- `make test_attention NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0` passes.
- `make test_layernorm NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0` passes.
- `make train_llama3cu NO_MULTI_GPU=1 NO_USE_MPI=1 FORCE_NVCC_O=0` passes.
- Launch scripts pass `bash -n`.
- Python helpers/data files pass `python3 -m py_compile`.
- Runtime validation is blocked in this environment by unavailable GPU access
  and missing reference checkpoint/debug-state binaries.

## Optional

- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): append-only history
- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): full documentation bundle
- [.claude/skills/](.claude/skills/): repo-local agent skills
